name: conductor-implement
description: Execute tasks from a track's implementation plan following TDD workflow
capabilities: Execute tasks from track implementation plans following defined workflow rules
Apply TDD workflow with Red-Green-Refactor phases when enabled
Track progress through phases with verification checkpoints between each phase
Update track status files and metadata.json with real-time progress
Handle errors with explicit user options instead of automatic continuation
Generate completion summaries and offer documentation sync options
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Security audit evaluated 82 static findings for external commands, 6 high-severity cryptographic warnings, and 3 network reconnaissance alerts. All findings were determined to be false positives. The SKILL.md file is a markdown documentation file containing workflow instructions, not executable code. Backtick patterns are markdown code block syntax, not Ruby shell execution. No actual security risks exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Struggling to maintain consistent development workflows across project tracks? This skill enforces structured TDD implementation with phase-based verification and progress tracking.
userTitle: Implement track tasks with TDD workflow
limitations: Requires Conductor initialization with product.md, workflow.md, and tracks.md files
Cannot execute without existing track structure in conductor/tracks/ directory
Waits for explicit user approval between phases - cannot run unattended
Limited to tracks that follow the conductor metadata format specification

useCases.title: Feature Development with TDD
useCases.description: Implement new features by following structured TDD workflow with automatic test creation, implementation, and refactoring phases tracked through completion.

useCases.targetUser: Engineers maintaining stable codebases with audit trails
useCases.title: Bug Fix Track Execution
useCases.description: Work through navigation bug fixes or similar issues with documented steps, verification checkpoints, and commit tracking for rollback capability.

useCases.targetUser: Tech leads managing structured development workflows
useCases.title: Multi-Phase Project Implementation
useCases.description: Execute complex multi-phase projects like user authentication systems with phase-based approval gates ensuring quality at each milestone.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: Start a track
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement the auth_20250115 track using the TDD workflow
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new track for the first time

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: List available tracks
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me which tracks are available to work on
promptTemplates.scenario: Selecting from incomplete tracks without specifying an ID

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Resume paused work
promptTemplates.prompt: Resume the dashboard_20250113 track from where I left off

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Task-specific implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement task 2.3 from the auth_20250115 track with strict TDD mode
promptTemplates.scenario: Targeting a specific task within a phase

bestPractices: Always verify Conductor initialization before starting a track - missing configuration files will cause workflow failures
Review and approve each phase verification checkpoint before proceeding to maintain quality gates
Keep plan.md status markers synchronized with actual progress - update [~] to [x] immediately after task completion
antiPatterns: Skipping verification checkpoints between phases - this bypasses quality assurance and risks accumulating undetected issues
Continuing implementation past errors without explicit resolution - always halt and select a recovery option
Manual file edits outside Conductor tracking - this causes git conflicts and breaks metadata.json state accuracy

faq.question: What happens if I close the session mid-track?
faq.answer: Progress is persisted in metadata.json. When you resume, the skill loads your last in-progress task and offers to continue, restart the task, or show a progress summary.

faq.question: Can I run this skill without TDD mode?
faq.answer: Yes. The TDD strictness level is configured in workflow.md. When TDD is not strict, the skill implements tasks directly and runs existing tests for verification instead of the full Red-Green-Refactor cycle.

faq.question: How do I revert changes if something goes wrong?
faq.answer: All commits are recorded in metadata.json with their hashes. You can use git revert with those commit hashes to undo specific task changes. The error handling menu also offers rollback options during implementation.

faq.question: What files does this skill modify?
faq.answer: The skill modifies conductor/tracks/{trackId}/plan.md for task status, conductor/tracks/{trackId}/metadata.json for progress tracking, conductor/tracks.md for overall track status, and your project source files during implementation.

faq.question: Can I run multiple tracks simultaneously?
faq.answer: The skill tracks one active track at a time. Starting a new track while another is in progress will mark the previous track appropriately. It is recommended to complete or pause one track before starting another.

faq.question: What is the cleanup option after track completion?
faq.answer: After completion you can archive the track to conductor/tracks/_archive/, delete the track directory entirely, or keep it in place. Archived tracks are preserved for reference without cluttering active tracks.
